VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
Summary:
BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION has made the second monitoring period verification of the 708 MW
BAESA project Hydro Power Plant (HPP), located in the Southern Region of Brazil, between the cities of Anita
Garibaldi, State of Santa Catarina and Pinhal da Serra, State of Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil.
The verification scope is defined as a periodic independent and objective review and an ex-post determination of
the monitored reductions in GHG emissions during defined verification period and consisted of the following
three phases: i) desk review of the project design, baseline and monitoring plan; ii) follow-up interviews with
project stakeholders: iii) resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final verification report and
opinion. The overall verification, from Contract Review to verification Report & Opinion, was conducted using
Bureau Veritas Certification internal procedures.
The first output of the verification process is a list of Clarification and Corrective Actions Requests (CL and
CAR), presented in the Verification Report. Taking into account this output, the project proponent revised its
Monitoring Report Document (Version 04). In summary, it is BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION’s opinion that
the project correctly applies the baseline and monitoring methodology ACM0002 version 07 and meets the
relevant VCS v.3 requirements, and local legislation. In the course of the verification, Corrective Action
Requests (CAR) and Clarification Requests (CL) were raised and successfully closed.
As the result of the 2nd periodic verification, the verifier confirms that the GHG emission reductions are calculated
without material misstatements in a conservative and appropriate manner. BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION
herewith confirms that the project has achieved emission reductions in the above mentioned reporting period as
follows:

VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
1 INTRODUCTION
1.1 Objective
Energética Barra Grande S/A BAESA has commissioned Bureau Veritas Certification to verify the
emission reductions of its VCS “BAESA Project”.
This report summarizes the findings of the second periodic verification of the project, performed on the
basis of VCS – Verified Carbon Standard version 3 criteria, as well as criteria given to provide for
consistent project operations, monitoring and reporting.
Verification is the periodic independent review and ex-post determination of the monitored reductions in
GHG emissions during defined verification period.
The objective of the periodic verification is to verify that actual monitoring systems and procedures are in
compliance with the monitoring systems and procedures described in the monitoring plan; furthermore,
the periodic verification evaluates the GHG emission reductions data and express a conclusion with a
high, but not absolute, level of assurance about whether the reported GHG emission data is free of
material misstatements; and verifies that the reported GHG emission reductions data is sufficiently
supported by evidence, i.e. monitoring records.
The verification shall consider both quantitative and qualitative information on emission reductions.
Quantitative data comprises the monitoring reports submitted to the verifier by the project entity.
Qualitative data comprises information on internal management controls, calculation procedures and
procedures for transfer, frequency of emissions reports and review of calculations/data transfers.
The verification is based on criteria set by VCS – Verified Carbon Standard version 3.
1.2 Scope and Criteria
The verification scope is defined as an independent and objective review of the project description, the
project’s baseline study and monitoring plan and other relevant documents. The information in these
documents is reviewed against VCS – Verified Carbon Standard version 3.
The verification is not meant to provide any consulting towards the Client. However, stated requests for
forward actions and/or corrective actions may provide input for improvement of the project monitoring
towards reductions in the GHG emissions.
v3.0 4
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
1.3 Level of assurance
During the verification process, the GHG team assessed the monitoring report, the calculation
spreadsheets and supporting documents.
Based on this assessment, the verification team can express a reasonable level of assurance, stating that
the GHG emission reductions:
- Are materially correct and are a fair representation of the GHG data, and
- Are prepared in accordance with the relevant International Standard on GHG
quantification, monitoring and reporting, and with relevant national standards.
1.4 Summary Description of the Project
The project activity consists on the supply of clean hydroelectric energy to the Brazilian National
Interconnected System (SIN) through the implantation and operation of Hydro Power Plant (HPP) Barra
Grande, located in the Southern Region of Brazil, between the cities of Anita Garibaldi, state of Santa
Catarina and Pinhal da Serra, state of Rio Grande do Sul, with an installed capacity of 708 MW, using a
small reservoir, with low environmental impact related to its installed capacity.
Baseline scenario establishes that electricity delivered to the grid by the project activity would have been
generated otherwise by the operation of a grid-connected power plant and by the addition of new
generating sources, as reflected in the combined margin (CM) calculations described in the “Tool to
calculate the emission factor for an electricity system”.
The alternative scenarios to the project activity are two situations which reflects the calculation of the
combined margin of baseline scenario: (i) the continuity of the present scenario, with electricity generation
happening according to the current generation composition of the National Interconnected System, more
specifically of the South Subsystem and (ii) the construction of a new mineral coal thermoelectric power
plant.
BAESA Project makes use of three generator units with three specific meters (for each unit), destined to
measure the Project’s generation of energy. The project activity reduces the emission of greenhouse
gases (GHGs) through the use of renewable sources of energy and making use of clean technology,
avoiding the generation of electricity via sources of fossils fuels with consequent emissions of CO2, that
would be generated if the project did not exist. The reservoir of HPP Barra Grande is responsible for
emissions of CH4 which were accounted for and reduced from the total project emission reduction.
The Crediting period is from 01/01/2006 to 31/12/2015. Project crediting period is 10 years renewed at
most two times, while the project star date is 11/01/2005 (Operation Starting Date of the First Turbine).
The emission reductions due to the renewable energy generated and dispatched to the grid by Baesa
project resulted in 1,112,212 tCO e, during this 2nd monitoring period, from 01st January, 2009 to 31st
2
December, 2010.

2.3 Validation Conclusion
According to the Validation Report N°BRAZIL-VAL/03615/2008 Version 03, issued in February 9th 2009,
by Bureau Veritas Certification Holding SAS:
Bureau Veritas Certification has performed the Validation of “BAESA Project” Hydro Power Plant (HPP)
Barra Grande, located in the Southern Region of Brazil, between the cities of Anita Garibaldi, state of
Santa Catarina and Pinhal da Serra, state of Rio Grande do Sul, with an installed capacity of 708 MW,
using a small reservoir, with low environmental impact related to its installed capacity.
The validation was performed on the basis of VCS criteria and host country criteria and also on the
criteria given to provide for consistent project operations, monitoring and reporting. The validation
consisted of the following three phases:
i) A desk review of the project design and the baseline and monitoring plan (October 2008);
ii) Follow-up interviews and on site visit (October 2008);
iii) The resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final validation report and opinion
(February 2009).
Project participants used the latest tool for demonstration of the additionality. In line with this tool, the PD
provides analysis of investment and prevailing barriers to determine that the project activity itself is not
the baseline scenario.
By generating electricity using renewable energy, the project is likely to result in reductions of GHG
emissions partially displacing the electricity that would have been generated using fossil fuels. An
analysis of the investment barrier demonstrates that the proposed project activity is not a likely baseline
scenario. Emission reductions attributable to the project are hence additional to any that would occur in
the absence of the project activity. Given that the project is implemented and maintained as designed, the
project is likely to achieve the estimated amount of emission reductions. The review of the Project
Description (Version 01 dated August 27, 2008) and the subsequent follow-up interviews have provided
Bureau Veritas Certification with sufficient evidence to determine the fulfillment of stated criteria. The
Project Description was subsequently revised as Version 02 dated October 20, 2008, Version 03 dated
November 20, 2008 and Version 04 dated February 03, 2009 to resolve the issues that rose during the
interviews and subsequent interactions.

: VCS Version 3
In our opinion, the project correctly applies and meets the relevant VCS requirements and the relevant
host country criteria. The validation is based on the information made available to us and the engagement
conditions detailed in this report.
3 VERIFICATION PROCESS
3.1 Method and Criteria
This second verification comprises the period from 01st January, 2009 to 31st December, 2010.
Thus verification report were prepared between 25th October, 2011 and 22nd March, 2012 . This period
encompass the site visit preparation, site visit, preliminary report, final report, internal technical review
(ITR) and final report adjustments.
The criteria of this verification include the relevant rules and steps as set out in the VCS v.3.
The verification process includes
 A desk review of the VCS PD and supporting documents with the use of the relevant sections of a
customized protocol according to the VVM 01.2 ;
 A desk review of the Monitoring Report (MR) and additional supporting documents which the
client submitted. The relevant sections of the above-mentioned customized protocol according to
the VVM 01.2 were use;
 On-Site assessment;
 Background investigation and follow-up interviews with personnel of the project developer;
 Verification reporting (Draft Verification Report and Final Verification Report).
3.2 Document Review
The document review was conducted based in a desk review of the Monitoring Report (MR), calculation
spreadsheets and additional supporting documents submitted by the client. The relevant sections of the
above-mentioned customized protocol according to the VVM 01.2 were used.
Also the desk review of the VCS PD, supporting documents and methodology ACM0002 version 07 were
done by using the relevant sections of a customized protocol according to the VVM 01.2.
All the relevant information and evidences regarding project description and monitoring process were
assessed and validated by the verification team during the site visit and documentation review.

3.4 Site Inspections
On-Site assessment was conducted in order to crosscheck all the relevant information and data provided
by the PP, against to the physical documentation and registers, as well as to assess the project
description, as per the MR and PD, to that observed in the field.
3.5 Resolution of Any Material Discrepancy
During the verification process some corrective action requests (CAR) and clarification requests (CL).
identified by the verifier were raised in the verification protocol and submitted to the PP to be addressed
before the conclusion of the verification process.
The resolution of CARs and CLs were carried out by the PP through adjustments in the project
documentation, especially in the MR and calculus spreadsheet. In some cases this adjustments were
supported by evidences, additional documentation or technical explanation.
No FAR was raised in the previous verification.
The objective of this phase of the verification is to raise the requests for corrective actions and
clarification and any other outstanding issues that needed to be clarified for Bureau Veritas Certification
positive conclusion on the GHG emission reduction calculation.
Findings established during the initial verification can either be seen as a non-fulfilment of criteria
ensuring the proper implementation of a project or where a risk to deliver high quality emission reductions
is identified.
v3.0 11
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
Corrective Action Requests (CAR) is issued, where:
(a) Non-conformities with the monitoring plan or methodology are found in monitoring and reporting, or if
the evidence provided to prove conformity is insufficient;
(b) Mistakes have been made in applying assumptions, data or calculations of emission reductions which
would impair the estimate of emission reductions;
The Clarification Request (CL) is used if information is insufficient or not clear enough to determine
whether the applicable VCS v.3 requirements have been met.
To guarantee the transparency of the verification process, the concerns raised are documented in more
detail in the verification protocol found in the section 7, below.
4 VERIFICATION FINDINGS
4.1 Project Implementation Status
There are no pending CARs or CLs issued in the course of the verification that still needs to be properly
addressed to get closed. Before issuance of the final verification report, these CARs and CLs were
adequately answered by the project proponent, and are closed.
The implementation status of the project is as follows:
The project has a 10-year crediting period, twice renewable.
The first 10-year crediting period is from 01st January 2006 and ends on 31st December 2015. The total
Emission Reductions forecasted for that period are 3,187,939 tCO2e.
This is the second monitoring period, from 01st January, 2009 to 31st December, 2010, where the
emission reductions were calculated in 1,112,212 tCO2e.
The previous monitoring period encompassed the period from 01st January 2006 to 31st December 2008,
generating 1,028,338 tCO2e.
The BAESA Project start date is 11th January 2005, that refers to the Operation Starting Date of the First
Turbine.
Project activity is in commercial operation since 11/01/2005. The amount of net electricity generation in
2009 was 2,984 GWh and in 2010 was 4,164 GWh.
The monitoring plan is in accordance with the approved methodology applied by the proposed VCS
project activity.
The parameters required by the monitoring plan and the way the Verification Team has verified the values
in the Monitoring Report Version 04 are described below:

4.2 Accuracy of GHG Emission Reduction or Removal Calculations
The parameters used for the emission reduction calculation are: Electricity Supplied to the Grid – Net
Electricity (EGy), EF - Combined Margin CO Emission Factor - and TEGy - Total electricity
grid,CM,y 2
produced by the project activity -.
For the first parameter (EGy), according to the technical specifications defined by ONS Grid Procedures,
in HPP Barra Grande there is a SMF - Measurement Billing System. The communication of this SMF with
CCEE occurs directly through a VPN (Virtual Private Network) and, consequently, SCDE - Electric Power
Data Collection System for billing - has direct access to the mass memory of the electricity meters.
v3.0 18
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
For the second parameter (EF ), the input values (operating margin and build margin emission
grid,CM,y
factors) are obtained from the official reports, issued periodically by the Brazilian DNA
(http://www.mct.gov.br/index.php/content/view/72764.html).
The third parameter: TEGy (Total electricity produced by the project activity) is used to calculate the
project emission. This parameter was crosschecked against Baesa internal official records (monthly
records). Comparison between TEGy and EGy monthly values was done in order to identify any
inconsistence.
In the first week of each month, HPP Barra Grande operators collect, in the electricity meters mass
memory, gross and net hourly generation data of the generation units referring to the previous month.
This data are compiled in a unique spreadsheet which is the HPP’s internal control generation
spreadsheet for the referred month under analysis.
Information of the internal control generation spreadsheet is checked with SCDE spreadsheets. However,
for emission reductions calculation, it was used the electricity generation data of CCEE General
Measurement Report, available in the CCEE website. This data consist of the net electricity generation
minus the losses of National Interconnected System (approximately 2.5%), setting, therefore, a more
conservative value.
Both generation information, internally generated and through CCEE website, are electronically stored by
the Operation and Maintenance Manager.
Periodically, the Information Technology Area makes a backup for all plant data through backup tapes.
All data collected as part of the monitoring was archived and will be kept for at least 2 years after the end
of the last crediting period.
All the information related to the total electricity produced by the project activity, including the electricity
supplied to the grid and the electricity supplied to internal loads, in year y (MWh), the net energy supplied
to the grid and the calculation of the emission factors were checked by the validation team, according to
the monitoring plan.
The verification team had access and verified the Calibration Certificates of all the energy measurement
equipments.
The emission reductions for the second monitoring period were calculated as follow:
EF = EF X W + EF X W (Equation 1)
grid,CM,y grid,OM,y OM grid,BM,y BM
Where:
EF = Build margin CO2 emission factor in year y (tCO2e/ MWh)
grid,BM,y
EF = Operating Margin CO2 emission in year y (tCO2e/ MWh)
grid,OM,y
W = Weighting of operating margin emissions factor (%) (0.5, as per tool for emission factor
OM
calculation)
W = Weighting of build margin emissions factor (%) (0.5, as per tool for emission factor calculation)
BM
PEy = EFRes * TEGy / 1000 (Equation 2)

Where:
PEy = Emission from reservoir expressed as tCO2e/year;
EFRes = is the default emission factor for emissions from reservoirs, and the default value as per EB23 is
90 Kg CO2e/MWh;
TEGy = Total electricity produced by the project activity, including the electricity supplied to the grid and
the electricity supplied to internal loads, in year y (MWh).
ERy = BEy – PEy (Equation 3)
Where:
ERy = Emission Reduction in year y (t CO2e/yr)
BEy = Baseline emissions in year y (t CO2e/yr)
PEy = Project emissions in year y (t CO2e/yr)
BEy = (EGy – EGbaseline) * EFgrid,CM,y (Equation 4)
Where:
BEy = Baseline Emission in year y (t CO2e/year)
EGy = Electricity supplied by the project activity to the grid (MWh)
EGbaseline = Baseline electricity supplied to the grid in case of modified or retrofit facilities (MWh). For
new power plants this value is taken as zero.
EFgrid,CM,y = Combined margin CO2 emission factor for grid connected power generation in year y
calculated using the latest version of the “Tool to calculate the emission factor for an electricity system”.
The two parameters (EGy - Electricity Supplied to the Grid – Net Electricity and EF - Combined
grid,CM,y

verification team against the official records from CCEE (monthly records) and Brazilian DNA (monthly

emission, was crosschecked against Baesa internal records (monthly records). For the three main

5 VERIFICATION CONCLUSION
Energética Barra Grande S.A – BAESA has commissioned BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION to carry
out the 2nd periodic verification of the Baesa project with regard to the relevant requirements for VCS
project activities. The project reduces GHG emissions by supplying clean hydroelectric energy to the
Brazilian National Interconnected System (SIN), that in a scenario without the project activity would have
been generated by the operation of a grid-connected power plant and by the addition of new generating
sources, some of them based in fossil fuel.
This verification covers the period from 01st January, 2009 to 31st December, 2010.
In the course of the verification, Corrective Action Requests (CAR) and Clarification Requests (CL) were
raised and successfully closed. There were 01 CAR and 02 CL raised. No FARs were raised. The
verification conclusion is based on the second Monitoring Report (Version 4, dated 15th March, 2012), the
VCS PD (Version 04, dated 03rd February, 2009), Emission Reduction Calculation spreadsheet v04 and
supporting documents made available to BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION by the project participant.
As a result of this verification, the DOE confirms that:
• BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION confirms that the project is implemented as planned and
described in the Project Description. The monitoring plan is in place and the project is generating GHG
emission reductions. The DOE concludes that all operations of the project are implemented and installed
as planned and described as in the validated project description;
• The monitoring plan is in accordance with the applied approved CDM methodology, i.e., ACM0002
"Consolidated baseline methodology for grid-connected electricity generation from renewable sources”,
version 7, valid from 14th December 2007 to 04th December 2008;
• The monitoring system is in place and functional. The project has generated GHG emission reductions.
BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION confirms that Monitoring Report v04 and Emission Reduction
Calculation spreadsheet v04 are in accordance with the registered monitoring plan and all the
calculations were based in reliable monitored data, in the case of non-monitored and pre-defined
parameters, has correctly followed the parameters stated in the validated VCS PD Version 04, dated 03rd
February, 2009.
The DOE also confirms that all the data and information used to elaborate this Verification Report are
treated confidentially by BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION.

4. Outros Elementos
Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
A descrição dos indicadores e metas relacionados à emissões de gases de efeito estufa, bem como a performance
da companhia em 2014, encontra-se nos itens 5.2 e 5.3.
4.2 Descrição de indicadores de emissão de GEE para as atividades da organização. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
Como parte da Estratégia Global de Sustentabilidade 2020/2030, a Alcoa acompanha o indicador de intensidade de
emissões (Toneladas de CO2eq/toneladas de alumínio produzido) referente às operações de primários (Processo de
Refinaria e Redução), responsáveis pela maior parte das emissões de gases de efeito estufa da companhia. Estes
indicadores estão relacionados com as metas 2020/2030 para redução da intensidade de emissões nas operações
de primários (metas descritas na seção 5.3 abaixo).
Indicadores:
- Intensidade de Emissões Operação de Primários (Refinaria e Redução): (Toneladas de CO2eq/toneladas de
alumínio produzido)
Ano base 2005: 4,50
Status 2014: 3,79
Meta 2020: 3,15
Meta 2030: 2,93
- Intensidade de Emissões Operação de Redução (Toneladas de CO2eq/toneladas de alumínio produzido)
Ano base 2005: 3,41
Status 2014: 2,86
Meta 2020: 2,39
Meta 2030: 2,22
- Intensidade de Emissões Operação de Refinaria: (Toneladas de CO2eq/toneladas de alumina produzida)
Ano base 2005: 0,57
Status 2014: 0,49
Meta 2020: 0,40
Meta 2030: 0,37
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
- Metas Globais de Sustentabilidade 2020/2030
A Alcoa possui Metas Globais de Sustentabilidade 2020/2030 que auxiliam as unidades da companhia a integrar os
aspectos de sustentabilidade em suas operações. Dentre elas está a meta de redução de intensidade de emissões
(toneladas de CO2 equivalente/tonelada de produto). Abaixo apresenta-se a descrição da meta e o seu status em
2014:
Meta: "Reduzir em 30% o total (direta e indireta) da intensidade de emissões de dióxido de carbono equivalente
(CO2) na unidade de negócios de Produtos Primários até 2020; e 35% até 2030, comparando-se com o ano base de
2005"
Status 2014: Redução de 16%
Apesar de melhorias como a substituição de óleo combustível por GLP nas empilhadeiras de Poços e por gás
natural nos aquecedores de Tubarão, a maior redução no valor absoluto de emissões das plantas de Poços e
10/10/2019 12:43 14
Inventário 2014 - Alcoa Alumínio S/A
Alumar se deve a redução da produção de alumínio primário, com o fechamento de duas, das três linhas e o
fechamento da Redução na Alumar e Poços respectivamente. Isto impactou diretamente nos escopos 1 e 2 e
indiretamente no escopo 3, já que não houve uma redução expressiva no transporte de insumos para estas plantas.
No CENU, a diminuição na combustão móvel se deve à venda da aeronave da empresa e na diminuição de dois para
um andar do escritório corporativo.
Em Tubarão é importante ressaltar que em 2014 houve o início do funcionamento da Prensa IV, aumentando as
emissões de combustão estacionária da planta.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventários de GEE e/ou ao envio de informações relacionadas.
Não foi relatado.
4.5 Informações sobre incertezas, exclusões de fontes de dados e outras características da elaboração do
inventário.
Por limitações do sistema de Registro Público de Emissões, não é possível especificar a participação societária por
unidade de negócio (administração, refinaria e redução) do Consórcio de Alumínio do Maranhão, por estarem
debaixo do mesmo CNPJ. Cada unidade de negócio foi considerada uma unidade da controlada Consórcio de
Alumínio do Maranhão. As participações societárias são: Administração (42,40%), Refinaria (32,30%) e Redução
(60,0%).
Não foi reportado as emissões por biomassa no inventário da Mineração Rio do Norte, pois o mesmo já foi
descontado das emissões totais previamente pela consultoria que realizou o trabalho.
Por fim, existe a incerteza relacionada ao cálculo realizado pelo sistema do Registro Púlico de Emissões após a
inserção dos dados de emissões. Esta incerteza é inerente ao sistema que considera o arredondamento para um
número de casas decimais menor do que a ferramenta de cálculo providenciada pelo Programa Brasileiro GHG
Protocol, causando uma pequena diferença na casa decimal do centésimo. Por ora, não há uma alternativa ou
ajustes realizados no sistema para que a variação seja nula. Apesar disso, esta variação não influencia
significativamente o valor dos dados das companhias que publicam as suas emissões no Registro Público.
4.6 Descrição sobre ações internas para melhoria da qualidade do inventário de GEE. Por exemplo, sistematização
da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.
São utilizadas as planilhas de coleta e cálculo fornecidda pelo Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol, que foram
disponibilizadas no sharepoint interno e preenchido pelo próprio responsável da localidade. Planilhas internas
foram desenvolvidas para melhorar o processo de auditoria no almoxarifado da unidades da Alumar.

4. Outros Elementos
Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
Devido a dificuldade em encontrar informações de empresas do mesmo porte e semelhança com a operação da
UHE Barra Grande, foi adotado uma meta interna de redução de 5% das emissões diretas (escopo 1 e 2),
considerando ano base de 2013 (emissões diretas totalizaram 35,79 tCO2 e).
Para o ano de 2014 o escopo 1 e 2 registraram 33,80 t CO2e., desconsiderando a emissão acidental do SF6. Isso
equivale a uma redução de emissões diretas de 5,5%, atingindo a meta.
4.2 Descrição de indicadores de emissão de GEE para as atividades da organização. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
Foi adotado o seguinte indicador: emissões diretas (escopo 1 + escopo 2) / 100 MW de capacidade instalada (a UHE
Barra Grande possui capacidade instalada de 690MW).
Valor de 2013: 5,19 tCO2e/100 MW
Valor de 2014: 8,34 tCO2e/ 100MW. Se desconsiderarmos a emissão de SF6 (vazamento acidental) em 2014 o
indicador fica em 4,90 tCO2e/100MW.
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
Todos os colaboradores participam de treinamentos do Sistema de Gestão Integrado, onde são abordados assuntos
como redução do consumo de combustíveis e emissões atmosféricas.
Além disso, foi adotada uma meta interna de redução de 5% das emissões diretas, conforme descrito no item 5.1.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventários de GEE e/ou ao envio de informações relacionadas.
Os novos contratos possuem requisitos para informação do consumo de combustível dos prestadores de serviços.
4.5 Informações sobre incertezas, exclusões de fontes de dados e outras características da elaboração do
inventário.
A verificação do inventário foi realizada por terceira parte, onde as discrepâncias foram corrigidas durante o
processo de auditoria.
Em função da ausência de metodologias reconhecidas, não foram reportadas as emissões do reservatório da usina.
4.6 Descrição sobre ações internas para melhoria da qualidade do inventário de GEE. Por exemplo, sistematização
da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.
Melhoria na captação de dados de escopo 3, devido a obrigatoriedade de prestadores de serviços enviarem
informações mensais referente a consumo de combustível.
4.7 Informações sobre a compra de energia elétrica oriunda de fonte renovável.

VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
Summary:
BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION has made the second monitoring period verification of the 708 MW
BAESA project Hydro Power Plant (HPP), located in the Southern Region of Brazil, between the cities of Anita
Garibaldi, State of Santa Catarina and Pinhal da Serra, State of Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil.
The verification scope is defined as a periodic independent and objective review and an ex-post determination of
the monitored reductions in GHG emissions during defined verification period and consisted of the following
three phases: i) desk review of the project design, baseline and monitoring plan; ii) follow-up interviews with
project stakeholders: iii) resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final verification report and
opinion. The overall verification, from Contract Review to verification Report & Opinion, was conducted using
Bureau Veritas Certification internal procedures.
The first output of the verification process is a list of Clarification and Corrective Actions Requests (CL and
CAR), presented in the Verification Report. Taking into account this output, the project proponent revised its
Monitoring Report Document (Version 04). In summary, it is BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION’s opinion that
the project correctly applies the baseline and monitoring methodology ACM0002 version 07 and meets the
relevant VCS v.3 requirements, and local legislation. In the course of the verification, Corrective Action
Requests (CAR) and Clarification Requests (CL) were raised and successfully closed.
As the result of the 2nd periodic verification, the verifier confirms that the GHG emission reductions are calculated
without material misstatements in a conservative and appropriate manner. BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION
herewith confirms that the project has achieved emission reductions in the above mentioned reporting period as
follows:

VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
1 INTRODUCTION
1.1 Objective
Energética Barra Grande S/A BAESA has commissioned Bureau Veritas Certification to verify the
emission reductions of its VCS “BAESA Project”.
This report summarizes the findings of the second periodic verification of the project, performed on the
basis of VCS – Verified Carbon Standard version 3 criteria, as well as criteria given to provide for
consistent project operations, monitoring and reporting.
Verification is the periodic independent review and ex-post determination of the monitored reductions in
GHG emissions during defined verification period.
The objective of the periodic verification is to verify that actual monitoring systems and procedures are in
compliance with the monitoring systems and procedures described in the monitoring plan; furthermore,
the periodic verification evaluates the GHG emission reductions data and express a conclusion with a
high, but not absolute, level of assurance about whether the reported GHG emission data is free of
material misstatements; and verifies that the reported GHG emission reductions data is sufficiently
supported by evidence, i.e. monitoring records.
The verification shall consider both quantitative and qualitative information on emission reductions.
Quantitative data comprises the monitoring reports submitted to the verifier by the project entity.
Qualitative data comprises information on internal management controls, calculation procedures and
procedures for transfer, frequency of emissions reports and review of calculations/data transfers.
The verification is based on criteria set by VCS – Verified Carbon Standard version 3.
1.2 Scope and Criteria
The verification scope is defined as an independent and objective review of the project description, the
project’s baseline study and monitoring plan and other relevant documents. The information in these
documents is reviewed against VCS – Verified Carbon Standard version 3.
The verification is not meant to provide any consulting towards the Client. However, stated requests for
forward actions and/or corrective actions may provide input for improvement of the project monitoring
towards reductions in the GHG emissions.
v3.0 4
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
1.3 Level of assurance
During the verification process, the GHG team assessed the monitoring report, the calculation
spreadsheets and supporting documents.
Based on this assessment, the verification team can express a reasonable level of assurance, stating that
the GHG emission reductions:
- Are materially correct and are a fair representation of the GHG data, and
- Are prepared in accordance with the relevant International Standard on GHG
quantification, monitoring and reporting, and with relevant national standards.
1.4 Summary Description of the Project
The project activity consists on the supply of clean hydroelectric energy to the Brazilian National
Interconnected System (SIN) through the implantation and operation of Hydro Power Plant (HPP) Barra
Grande, located in the Southern Region of Brazil, between the cities of Anita Garibaldi, state of Santa
Catarina and Pinhal da Serra, state of Rio Grande do Sul, with an installed capacity of 708 MW, using a
small reservoir, with low environmental impact related to its installed capacity.
Baseline scenario establishes that electricity delivered to the grid by the project activity would have been
generated otherwise by the operation of a grid-connected power plant and by the addition of new
generating sources, as reflected in the combined margin (CM) calculations described in the “Tool to
calculate the emission factor for an electricity system”.
The alternative scenarios to the project activity are two situations which reflects the calculation of the
combined margin of baseline scenario: (i) the continuity of the present scenario, with electricity generation
happening according to the current generation composition of the National Interconnected System, more
specifically of the South Subsystem and (ii) the construction of a new mineral coal thermoelectric power
plant.
BAESA Project makes use of three generator units with three specific meters (for each unit), destined to
measure the Project’s generation of energy. The project activity reduces the emission of greenhouse
gases (GHGs) through the use of renewable sources of energy and making use of clean technology,
avoiding the generation of electricity via sources of fossils fuels with consequent emissions of CO2, that
would be generated if the project did not exist. The reservoir of HPP Barra Grande is responsible for
emissions of CH4 which were accounted for and reduced from the total project emission reduction.
The Crediting period is from 01/01/2006 to 31/12/2015. Project crediting period is 10 years renewed at
most two times, while the project star date is 11/01/2005 (Operation Starting Date of the First Turbine).
The emission reductions due to the renewable energy generated and dispatched to the grid by Baesa
project resulted in 1,112,212 tCO e, during this 2nd monitoring period, from 01st January, 2009 to 31st
2
December, 2010.

2.3 Validation Conclusion
According to the Validation Report N°BRAZIL-VAL/03615/2008 Version 03, issued in February 9th 2009,
by Bureau Veritas Certification Holding SAS:
Bureau Veritas Certification has performed the Validation of “BAESA Project” Hydro Power Plant (HPP)
Barra Grande, located in the Southern Region of Brazil, between the cities of Anita Garibaldi, state of
Santa Catarina and Pinhal da Serra, state of Rio Grande do Sul, with an installed capacity of 708 MW,
using a small reservoir, with low environmental impact related to its installed capacity.
The validation was performed on the basis of VCS criteria and host country criteria and also on the
criteria given to provide for consistent project operations, monitoring and reporting. The validation
consisted of the following three phases:
i) A desk review of the project design and the baseline and monitoring plan (October 2008);
ii) Follow-up interviews and on site visit (October 2008);
iii) The resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final validation report and opinion
(February 2009).
Project participants used the latest tool for demonstration of the additionality. In line with this tool, the PD
provides analysis of investment and prevailing barriers to determine that the project activity itself is not
the baseline scenario.
By generating electricity using renewable energy, the project is likely to result in reductions of GHG
emissions partially displacing the electricity that would have been generated using fossil fuels. An
analysis of the investment barrier demonstrates that the proposed project activity is not a likely baseline
scenario. Emission reductions attributable to the project are hence additional to any that would occur in
the absence of the project activity. Given that the project is implemented and maintained as designed, the
project is likely to achieve the estimated amount of emission reductions. The review of the Project
Description (Version 01 dated August 27, 2008) and the subsequent follow-up interviews have provided
Bureau Veritas Certification with sufficient evidence to determine the fulfillment of stated criteria. The
Project Description was subsequently revised as Version 02 dated October 20, 2008, Version 03 dated
November 20, 2008 and Version 04 dated February 03, 2009 to resolve the issues that rose during the
interviews and subsequent interactions.

3 VERIFICATION PROCESS
3.1 Method and Criteria
This second verification comprises the period from 01st January, 2009 to 31st December, 2010.
Thus verification report were prepared between 25th October, 2011 and 22nd March, 2012 . This period
encompass the site visit preparation, site visit, preliminary report, final report, internal technical review
(ITR) and final report adjustments.
The criteria of this verification include the relevant rules and steps as set out in the VCS v.3.
The verification process includes
 A desk review of the VCS PD and supporting documents with the use of the relevant sections of a
customized protocol according to the VVM 01.2 ;
 A desk review of the Monitoring Report (MR) and additional supporting documents which the
client submitted. The relevant sections of the above-mentioned customized protocol according to
the VVM 01.2 were use;
 On-Site assessment;
 Background investigation and follow-up interviews with personnel of the project developer;
 Verification reporting (Draft Verification Report and Final Verification Report).
3.2 Document Review
The document review was conducted based in a desk review of the Monitoring Report (MR), calculation
spreadsheets and additional supporting documents submitted by the client. The relevant sections of the
above-mentioned customized protocol according to the VVM 01.2 were used.
Also the desk review of the VCS PD, supporting documents and methodology ACM0002 version 07 were
done by using the relevant sections of a customized protocol according to the VVM 01.2.
All the relevant information and evidences regarding project description and monitoring process were
assessed and validated by the verification team during the site visit and documentation review.

3.5 Resolution of Any Material Discrepancy
During the verification process some corrective action requests (CAR) and clarification requests (CL).
identified by the verifier were raised in the verification protocol and submitted to the PP to be addressed
before the conclusion of the verification process.
The resolution of CARs and CLs were carried out by the PP through adjustments in the project
documentation, especially in the MR and calculus spreadsheet. In some cases this adjustments were
supported by evidences, additional documentation or technical explanation.
No FAR was raised in the previous verification.
The objective of this phase of the verification is to raise the requests for corrective actions and
clarification and any other outstanding issues that needed to be clarified for Bureau Veritas Certification
positive conclusion on the GHG emission reduction calculation.
Findings established during the initial verification can either be seen as a non-fulfilment of criteria
ensuring the proper implementation of a project or where a risk to deliver high quality emission reductions
is identified.

VERIFICATION REPORT

Corrective Action Requests (CAR) is issued, where:
(a) Non-conformities with the monitoring plan or methodology are found in monitoring and reporting, or if
the evidence provided to prove conformity is insufficient;
(b) Mistakes have been made in applying assumptions, data or calculations of emission reductions which
would impair the estimate of emission reductions;
The Clarification Request (CL) is used if information is insufficient or not clear enough to determine
whether the applicable VCS v.3 requirements have been met.
To guarantee the transparency of the verification process, the concerns raised are documented in more
detail in the verification protocol found in the section 7, below.
4 VERIFICATION FINDINGS
4.1 Project Implementation Status
There are no pending CARs or CLs issued in the course of the verification that still needs to be properly
addressed to get closed. Before issuance of the final verification report, these CARs and CLs were
adequately answered by the project proponent, and are closed.
The implementation status of the project is as follows:
The project has a 10-year crediting period, twice renewable.
The first 10-year crediting period is from 01st January 2006 and ends on 31st December 2015. The total
Emission Reductions forecasted for that period are 3,187,939 tCO2e.
This is the second monitoring period, from 01st January, 2009 to 31st December, 2010, where the
emission reductions were calculated in 1,112,212 tCO2e.
The previous monitoring period encompassed the period from 01st January 2006 to 31st December 2008,
generating 1,028,338 tCO2e.
The BAESA Project start date is 11th January 2005, that refers to the Operation Starting Date of the First
Turbine.
Project activity is in commercial operation since 11/01/2005. The amount of net electricity generation in
2009 was 2,984 GWh and in 2010 was 4,164 GWh.
The monitoring plan is in accordance with the approved methodology applied by the proposed VCS
project activity.
The parameters required by the monitoring plan and the way the Verification Team has verified the values
in the Monitoring Report Version 04 are described below:

4.2 Accuracy of GHG Emission Reduction or Removal Calculations
The parameters used for the emission reduction calculation are: Electricity Supplied to the Grid – Net
Electricity (EGy), EF - Combined Margin CO Emission Factor - and TEGy - Total electricity
grid,CM,y 2
produced by the project activity -.
For the first parameter (EGy), according to the technical specifications defined by ONS Grid Procedures,
in HPP Barra Grande there is a SMF - Measurement Billing System. The communication of this SMF with
CCEE occurs directly through a VPN (Virtual Private Network) and, consequently, SCDE - Electric Power
Data Collection System for billing - has direct access to the mass memory of the electricity meters.
v3.0 18
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
For the second parameter (EF ), the input values (operating margin and build margin emission
grid,CM,y
factors) are obtained from the official reports, issued periodically by the Brazilian DNA
(http://www.mct.gov.br/index.php/content/view/72764.html).
The third parameter: TEGy (Total electricity produced by the project activity) is used to calculate the
project emission. This parameter was crosschecked against Baesa internal official records (monthly
records). Comparison between TEGy and EGy monthly values was done in order to identify any
inconsistence.
In the first week of each month, HPP Barra Grande operators collect, in the electricity meters mass
memory, gross and net hourly generation data of the generation units referring to the previous month.
This data are compiled in a unique spreadsheet which is the HPP’s internal control generation
spreadsheet for the referred month under analysis.
Information of the internal control generation spreadsheet is checked with SCDE spreadsheets. However,
for emission reductions calculation, it was used the electricity generation data of CCEE General
Measurement Report, available in the CCEE website. This data consist of the net electricity generation
minus the losses of National Interconnected System (approximately 2.5%), setting, therefore, a more
conservative value.
Both generation information, internally generated and through CCEE website, are electronically stored by
the Operation and Maintenance Manager.
Periodically, the Information Technology Area makes a backup for all plant data through backup tapes.
All data collected as part of the monitoring was archived and will be kept for at least 2 years after the end
of the last crediting period.
All the information related to the total electricity produced by the project activity, including the electricity
supplied to the grid and the electricity supplied to internal loads, in year y (MWh), the net energy supplied
to the grid and the calculation of the emission factors were checked by the validation team, according to
the monitoring plan.
The verification team had access and verified the Calibration Certificates of all the energy measurement
equipments.

grid,OM,y

OM
calculation)

4.3 Quality of Evidence to Determine GHG Emission Reductions or Removals
The project proponent as evidence to determine emission reductions submitted several documents, as
presented in section 3.2. above.
The evidences were assessed as of sufficient quantity and appropriate quality by DOE.
4.4 Management and Operational System
As presented in the Monitoring Report v04, section 3.3:
" HPP Barra Grande Operation and Maintenance Manager is responsible for the operation and
maintenance activities of the plant and for the activities of consolidation and analysis of gross and net
electricity. Measurements activities are carried out by the operators of the operation team".
Furthermore, the PP has explained that: "Project Participants performs cross-check of electricity
generation information and emission reductions calculations. BAESA has internal auditing process, once
the company is certified ISO 9001 and ISO 14001. However, the company did not establish a formal
procedure for emission reduction monitoring".

5 VERIFICATION CONCLUSION
Energética Barra Grande S.A – BAESA has commissioned BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION to carry
out the 2nd periodic verification of the Baesa project with regard to the relevant requirements for VCS
project activities. The project reduces GHG emissions by supplying clean hydroelectric energy to the
Brazilian National Interconnected System (SIN), that in a scenario without the project activity would have
been generated by the operation of a grid-connected power plant and by the addition of new generating
sources, some of them based in fossil fuel.
This verification covers the period from 01st January, 2009 to 31st December, 2010.
In the course of the verification, Corrective Action Requests (CAR) and Clarification Requests (CL) were
raised and successfully closed. There were 01 CAR and 02 CL raised. No FARs were raised. The
verification conclusion is based on the second Monitoring Report (Version 4, dated 15th March, 2012), the
VCS PD (Version 04, dated 03rd February, 2009), Emission Reduction Calculation spreadsheet v04 and
supporting documents made available to BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION by the project participant.
As a result of this verification, the DOE confirms that:
• BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION confirms that the project is implemented as planned and
described in the Project Description. The monitoring plan is in place and the project is generating GHG
emission reductions. The DOE concludes that all operations of the project are implemented and installed
as planned and described as in the validated project description;
• The monitoring plan is in accordance with the applied approved CDM methodology, i.e., ACM0002
"Consolidated baseline methodology for grid-connected electricity generation from renewable sources”,
version 7, valid from 14th December 2007 to 04th December 2008;
• The monitoring system is in place and functional. The project has generated GHG emission reductions.
BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION confirms that Monitoring Report v04 and Emission Reduction
Calculation spreadsheet v04 are in accordance with the registered monitoring plan and all the
calculations were based in reliable monitored data, in the case of non-monitored and pre-defined
parameters, has correctly followed the parameters stated in the validated VCS PD Version 04, dated 03rd
February, 2009.
The DOE also confirms that all the data and information used to elaborate this Verification Report are
treated confidentially by BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION.

4. Outros Elementos
Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
A descrição dos indicadores e metas relacionados à emissões de gases de efeito estufa, bem como a performance
da companhia em 2015, encontram-se nos itens abaixo. A única das três linhas da Redução da Alumar que ainda
estava em operação foi desligada em abril de 2015. Em decorrência das condições de operação que antecedem o
desligamento, assim como dos preparativos necessários, parâmetros de eficiência foram afetados, inclusive aqueles
relacionados a consumo de energia e emissão de GEE, resultando em um aumento de 10% na intensidade de
emissões em relação ao ano base de 2005.
4.2 Descrição de indicadores de emissão de GEE para as atividades da organização. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
Indicadores:
- Intensidade de Emissões Operação de Primários (Refinaria e Redução): (Toneladas de CO2eq/toneladas de
alumínio produzido)
Status 2015: 4,71
- Intensidade de Emissões Operação de Redução (Toneladas de CO2eq/toneladas de alumínio produzido)
Status 2015: 3,76
- Intensidade de Emissões Operação de Refinaria: (Toneladas de CO2eq/toneladas de alumina produzida)
Status 2015: 0,50
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
A Alcoa possui Metas Globais de Sustentabilidade 2020/2030 que auxiliam as unidades da companhia a integrar os
aspectos de sustentabilidade em suas operações. Dentre elas está a meta de redução de intensidade de emissões
(toneladas de CO2 equivalente/tonelada de produto). Abaixo apresenta-se a descrição da meta e o seu status em
2015:
Meta: "Reduzir em 30% o total (direta e indireta) da intensidade de emissões de dióxido de carbono equivalente
(CO2) na unidade de negócios de Produtos Primários até 2020; e 35% até 2030, comparando-se com o ano base de
2005"
Status 2015: Aumento de 10% em relação ao ano base de 2005
A substituição do óleo combustível pelo gás natural na fábrica de Poços foi consolidada no início de 2015, assim
como a implementação total do gás natural nos aquecedores de Tubarão. No entanto, a maior redução no valor
absoluto de emissões se deve ao desligamento da produção de alumínio primário em São Luis, com o fechamento
da última linha da Redução na Alumar. Isto impactou diretamente nos escopos 1 e 2 e indiretamente no escopo 3.
No CENU, a diminuição na combustão móvel se deve à venda da aeronave da empresa em 2014.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventários de GEE e/ou ao envio de informações relacionadas.
Não foi relatado.
28/11/2019 20:09 13
Inventário 2015 - Alcoa Alumínio S/A
4.5 Informações sobre incertezas, exclusões de fontes de dados e outras características da elaboração do
inventário.
Este ano, com a implementação do novo sistema do Registro Público, não foi possível desagregar os inventários da
Alumar por unidades de negócio. Assim, o cáculo da participação societária foi calculado em uma planilha à parte e
inseridas no sistema o valor consolidado. As participações societárias são: Administração (42,40%), Refinaria
(32,30%) e Redução (60,0%).
Por esta mesma razão, os dados de Poços de Caldas, foram considerados no inventário da Alcoa Alumínio S/A, já
que Poços é a matriz da Alcoa no Brasil.
Existe também a incerteza relacionada ao cálculo realizado pelo sistema do Registro Púlico de Emissões após a
inserção dos dados de emissões. Esta incerteza é inerente ao sistema que considera o arredondamento para um
número de casas decimais menor do que a ferramenta de cálculo providenciada pelo Programa Brasileiro GHG
Protocol, causando uma pequena diferença na casa decimal do centésimo. Por ora, não há uma alternativa ou
ajustes realizados no sistema para que a variação seja nula. Apesar disso, esta variação não influencia
significativamente o valor dos dados das companhias que publicam as suas emissões no Registro Público.
4.6 Descrição sobre ações internas para melhoria da qualidade do inventário de GEE. Por exemplo, sistematização
da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.
Como forma de melhoria, incluímos as emissões das empilhadeiras em combustão móvel e melhoramos a
classificação dos resíduos inertes. Este ano, o reporte da MRN foi realizado e auditado pela própria empresa
aumentando o grau de confiabilidade dos dados.
4.7 Informações sobre a compra de energia elétrica oriunda de fonte renovável.

Não foi relatado.

inventariado.

4. Outros Elementos
Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
Devido a dificuldade em encontrar informações de empresas do mesmo porte e semelhança com a operação da
UHE Barra Grande, foi
adotado uma meta interna de redução de 5% das emissões diretas (escopo 1 e 2), considerando ano base de 2013
(emissões diretas
totalizaram 35,79 tCO2 e).
Em 2014 houve redução de 5,5% de emissões, atingindo a meta.
Já em 2015 foi registrado um aumento de 4,27% das emissões.
4.2 Descrição de indicadores de emissão de GEE para as atividades da organização. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
Indicador adotado: emissões diretas (escopo 1 + escopo 2) / 100 MW de capacidade instalada*
* A UHE Barra Grande possui capacidade instalada de 690MW.
2013: 5,19 tCO2e/100 MW
2014: 8,34 tCO2e/ 100MW. Se desconsiderarmos a emissão de SF6 (vazamento acidental), o indicador fica em 4,90
tCO2e/100MW.
2015: 7,99 tCO2e/100MW. Desconsiderando o vazamento acidental de SF6, o indicador fica em 5,41
tCO2e/100MW.
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
Todos os colaboradores participam de treinamentos do Sistema de Gestão Integrado, onde são abordados assuntos
como redução do
consumo de combustíveis e emissões atmosféricas.
Além disso, foi adotada uma meta interna de redução de 5% das emissões de Escopo 1 e Escopo 2.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventários de GEE e/ou ao envio de informações relacionadas.
Os novos contratos possuem requisitos para informação do consumo de combustível dos prestadores de serviços.
4.5 Informações sobre incertezas, exclusões de fontes de dados e outras características da elaboração do
inventário.
A verificação do inventário foi realizada por terceira parte, onde as discrepâncias foram corrigidas durante o
processo de auditoria.
Em função da ausência de metodologias reconhecidas, não foram reportadas as emissões do reservatório da usina.
4.6 Descrição sobre ações internas para melhoria da qualidade do inventário de GEE. Por exemplo, sistematização
da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.

Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol
Esta Declaração de Verificação1 documenta que o Organismo de Verificação (OV) citado abaixo realizou as
atividades de verificação de acordo com as Especificações de Verificação do Programa Brasileiro GHG
Protocol e a norma ABNT NBR ISO 14064-3:2007.

Nome do OV: SGS ICS Certificadora Ltda. Nome da OI: BAESA Energética Barra Grande S.A.
Nome do verificador líder: Nicole Celupi Nome do responsável pelo inventário: Khadine Thatiane Appio

As emissões de gases de efeito estufa (GEE) informadas pela Organização Inventariante em seu inventário de
emissões, de 1º de janeiro até 31 de dezembro de 2015, são verificáveis e cumprem os requisitos do
Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol, detalhados nas Especificações do Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol de
Contabilização, Quantificação e Publicação de Inventários Corporativos de Emissões de Gases de Efeito Estufa
(EPB).
Nível de Confiança
O Organismo de Verificação (OV) atribuiu o seguinte nível de confiança ao processo de verificação:

Summary:
BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION has made the second monitoring period verification of the 708 MW
BAESA project Hydro Power Plant (HPP), located in the Southern Region of Brazil, between the cities of Anita
Garibaldi, State of Santa Catarina and Pinhal da Serra, State of Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil.
The verification scope is defined as a periodic independent and objective review and an ex-post determination of
the monitored reductions in GHG emissions during defined verification period and consisted of the following
three phases: i) desk review of the project design, baseline and monitoring plan; ii) follow-up interviews with
project stakeholders: iii) resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final verification report and
opinion. The overall verification, from Contract Review to verification Report & Opinion, was conducted using
Bureau Veritas Certification internal procedures.
The first output of the verification process is a list of Clarification and Corrective Actions Requests (CL and
CAR), presented in the Verification Report. Taking into account this output, the project proponent revised its
Monitoring Report Document (Version 04). In summary, it is BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION’s opinion that
the project correctly applies the baseline and monitoring methodology ACM0002 version 07 and meets the
relevant VCS v.3 requirements, and local legislation. In the course of the verification, Corrective Action
Requests (CAR) and Clarification Requests (CL) were raised and successfully closed.
As the result of the 2nd periodic verification, the verifier confirms that the GHG emission reductions are calculated
without material misstatements in a conservative and appropriate manner. BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION
herewith confirms that the project has achieved emission reductions in the above mentioned reporting period as
follows:

VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
1 INTRODUCTION
1.1 Objective
Energética Barra Grande S/A BAESA has commissioned Bureau Veritas Certification to verify the
emission reductions of its VCS “BAESA Project”.
This report summarizes the findings of the second periodic verification of the project, performed on the
basis of VCS – Verified Carbon Standard version 3 criteria, as well as criteria given to provide for
consistent project operations, monitoring and reporting.
Verification is the periodic independent review and ex-post determination of the monitored reductions in
GHG emissions during defined verification period.
The objective of the periodic verification is to verify that actual monitoring systems and procedures are in
compliance with the monitoring systems and procedures described in the monitoring plan; furthermore,
the periodic verification evaluates the GHG emission reductions data and express a conclusion with a
high, but not absolute, level of assurance about whether the reported GHG emission data is free of
material misstatements; and verifies that the reported GHG emission reductions data is sufficiently
supported by evidence, i.e. monitoring records.
The verification shall consider both quantitative and qualitative information on emission reductions.
Quantitative data comprises the monitoring reports submitted to the verifier by the project entity.
Qualitative data comprises information on internal management controls, calculation procedures and
procedures for transfer, frequency of emissions reports and review of calculations/data transfers.
The verification is based on criteria set by VCS – Verified Carbon Standard version 3.
1.2 Scope and Criteria
The verification scope is defined as an independent and objective review of the project description, the
project’s baseline study and monitoring plan and other relevant documents. The information in these
documents is reviewed against VCS – Verified Carbon Standard version 3.
The verification is not meant to provide any consulting towards the Client. However, stated requests for
forward actions and/or corrective actions may provide input for improvement of the project monitoring
towards reductions in the GHG emissions.
v3.0 4
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
1.3 Level of assurance
During the verification process, the GHG team assessed the monitoring report, the calculation
spreadsheets and supporting documents.
Based on this assessment, the verification team can express a reasonable level of assurance, stating that
the GHG emission reductions:
- Are materially correct and are a fair representation of the GHG data, and
- Are prepared in accordance with the relevant International Standard on GHG
quantification, monitoring and reporting, and with relevant national standards.
1.4 Summary Description of the Project
The project activity consists on the supply of clean hydroelectric energy to the Brazilian National
Interconnected System (SIN) through the implantation and operation of Hydro Power Plant (HPP) Barra
Grande, located in the Southern Region of Brazil, between the cities of Anita Garibaldi, state of Santa
Catarina and Pinhal da Serra, state of Rio Grande do Sul, with an installed capacity of 708 MW, using a
small reservoir, with low environmental impact related to its installed capacity.
Baseline scenario establishes that electricity delivered to the grid by the project activity would have been
generated otherwise by the operation of a grid-connected power plant and by the addition of new
generating sources, as reflected in the combined margin (CM) calculations described in the “Tool to
calculate the emission factor for an electricity system”.
The alternative scenarios to the project activity are two situations which reflects the calculation of the
combined margin of baseline scenario: (i) the continuity of the present scenario, with electricity generation
happening according to the current generation composition of the National Interconnected System, more
specifically of the South Subsystem and (ii) the construction of a new mineral coal thermoelectric power
plant.
BAESA Project makes use of three generator units with three specific meters (for each unit), destined to
measure the Project’s generation of energy. The project activity reduces the emission of greenhouse
gases (GHGs) through the use of renewable sources of energy and making use of clean technology,
avoiding the generation of electricity via sources of fossils fuels with consequent emissions of CO2, that
would be generated if the project did not exist. The reservoir of HPP Barra Grande is responsible for
emissions of CH4 which were accounted for and reduced from the total project emission reduction.
The Crediting period is from 01/01/2006 to 31/12/2015. Project crediting period is 10 years renewed at
most two times, while the project star date is 11/01/2005 (Operation Starting Date of the First Turbine).
The emission reductions due to the renewable energy generated and dispatched to the grid by Baesa
project resulted in 1,112,212 tCO e, during this 2nd monitoring period, from 01st January, 2009 to 31st
2
December, 2010.

2.3 Validation Conclusion
According to the Validation Report N°BRAZIL-VAL/03615/2008 Version 03, issued in February 9th 2009,
by Bureau Veritas Certification Holding SAS:
Bureau Veritas Certification has performed the Validation of “BAESA Project” Hydro Power Plant (HPP)
Barra Grande, located in the Southern Region of Brazil, between the cities of Anita Garibaldi, state of
Santa Catarina and Pinhal da Serra, state of Rio Grande do Sul, with an installed capacity of 708 MW,
using a small reservoir, with low environmental impact related to its installed capacity.
The validation was performed on the basis of VCS criteria and host country criteria and also on the
criteria given to provide for consistent project operations, monitoring and reporting. The validation
consisted of the following three phases:
i) A desk review of the project design and the baseline and monitoring plan (October 2008);
ii) Follow-up interviews and on site visit (October 2008);
iii) The resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final validation report and opinion
(February 2009).
Project participants used the latest tool for demonstration of the additionality. In line with this tool, the PD
provides analysis of investment and prevailing barriers to determine that the project activity itself is not
the baseline scenario.
By generating electricity using renewable energy, the project is likely to result in reductions of GHG
emissions partially displacing the electricity that would have been generated using fossil fuels. An
analysis of the investment barrier demonstrates that the proposed project activity is not a likely baseline
scenario. Emission reductions attributable to the project are hence additional to any that would occur in
the absence of the project activity. Given that the project is implemented and maintained as designed, the
project is likely to achieve the estimated amount of emission reductions. The review of the Project
Description (Version 01 dated August 27, 2008) and the subsequent follow-up interviews have provided
Bureau Veritas Certification with sufficient evidence to determine the fulfillment of stated criteria. The
Project Description was subsequently revised as Version 02 dated October 20, 2008, Version 03 dated
November 20, 2008 and Version 04 dated February 03, 2009 to resolve the issues that rose during the
interviews and subsequent interactions.

3 VERIFICATION PROCESS
3.1 Method and Criteria
This second verification comprises the period from 01st January, 2009 to 31st December, 2010.
Thus verification report were prepared between 25th October, 2011 and 22nd March, 2012 . This period
encompass the site visit preparation, site visit, preliminary report, final report, internal technical review
(ITR) and final report adjustments.
The criteria of this verification include the relevant rules and steps as set out in the VCS v.3.
The verification process includes
 A desk review of the VCS PD and supporting documents with the use of the relevant sections of a
customized protocol according to the VVM 01.2 ;
 A desk review of the Monitoring Report (MR) and additional supporting documents which the
client submitted. The relevant sections of the above-mentioned customized protocol according to
the VVM 01.2 were use;
 On-Site assessment;
 Background investigation and follow-up interviews with personnel of the project developer;
 Verification reporting (Draft Verification Report and Final Verification Report).
3.2 Document Review
The document review was conducted based in a desk review of the Monitoring Report (MR), calculation
spreadsheets and additional supporting documents submitted by the client. The relevant sections of the
above-mentioned customized protocol according to the VVM 01.2 were used.
Also the desk review of the VCS PD, supporting documents and methodology ACM0002 version 07 were
done by using the relevant sections of a customized protocol according to the VVM 01.2.
All the relevant information and evidences regarding project description and monitoring process were
assessed and validated by the verification team during the site visit and documentation review.

3.5 Resolution of Any Material Discrepancy
During the verification process some corrective action requests (CAR) and clarification requests (CL).
identified by the verifier were raised in the verification protocol and submitted to the PP to be addressed
before the conclusion of the verification process.
The resolution of CARs and CLs were carried out by the PP through adjustments in the project
documentation, especially in the MR and calculus spreadsheet. In some cases this adjustments were
supported by evidences, additional documentation or technical explanation.
No FAR was raised in the previous verification.
The objective of this phase of the verification is to raise the requests for corrective actions and
clarification and any other outstanding issues that needed to be clarified for Bureau Veritas Certification
positive conclusion on the GHG emission reduction calculation.
Findings established during the initial verification can either be seen as a non-fulfilment of criteria
ensuring the proper implementation of a project or where a risk to deliver high quality emission reductions
is identified.
v3.0 11
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
Corrective Action Requests (CAR) is issued, where:
(a) Non-conformities with the monitoring plan or methodology are found in monitoring and reporting, or if
the evidence provided to prove conformity is insufficient;
(b) Mistakes have been made in applying assumptions, data or calculations of emission reductions which
would impair the estimate of emission reductions;
The Clarification Request (CL) is used if information is insufficient or not clear enough to determine
whether the applicable VCS v.3 requirements have been met.
To guarantee the transparency of the verification process, the concerns raised are documented in more
detail in the verification protocol found in the section 7, below.
4 VERIFICATION FINDINGS
4.1 Project Implementation Status
There are no pending CARs or CLs issued in the course of the verification that still needs to be properly
addressed to get closed. Before issuance of the final verification report, these CARs and CLs were
adequately answered by the project proponent, and are closed.
The implementation status of the project is as follows:
The project has a 10-year crediting period, twice renewable.
The first 10-year crediting period is from 01st January 2006 and ends on 31st December 2015. The total
Emission Reductions forecasted for that period are 3,187,939 tCO2e.
This is the second monitoring period, from 01st January, 2009 to 31st December, 2010, where the
emission reductions were calculated in 1,112,212 tCO2e.
The previous monitoring period encompassed the period from 01st January 2006 to 31st December 2008,
generating 1,028,338 tCO2e.
The BAESA Project start date is 11th January 2005, that refers to the Operation Starting Date of the First
Turbine.
Project activity is in commercial operation since 11/01/2005. The amount of net electricity generation in
2009 was 2,984 GWh and in 2010 was 4,164 GWh.
The monitoring plan is in accordance with the approved methodology applied by the proposed VCS
project activity.

4.2 Accuracy of GHG Emission Reduction or Removal Calculations
The parameters used for the emission reduction calculation are: Electricity Supplied to the Grid – Net
Electricity (EGy), EF - Combined Margin CO Emission Factor - and TEGy - Total electricity
grid,CM,y 2
produced by the project activity -.
For the first parameter (EGy), according to the technical specifications defined by ONS Grid Procedures,
in HPP Barra Grande there is a SMF - Measurement Billing System. The communication of this SMF with
CCEE occurs directly through a VPN (Virtual Private Network) and, consequently, SCDE - Electric Power
Data Collection System for billing - has direct access to the mass memory of the electricity meters.
v3.0 18
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
For the second parameter (EF ), the input values (operating margin and build margin emission
grid,CM,y
factors) are obtained from the official reports, issued periodically by the Brazilian DNA
(http://www.mct.gov.br/index.php/content/view/72764.html).
The third parameter: TEGy (Total electricity produced by the project activity) is used to calculate the
project emission. This parameter was crosschecked against Baesa internal official records (monthly
records). Comparison between TEGy and EGy monthly values was done in order to identify any
inconsistence.
In the first week of each month, HPP Barra Grande operators collect, in the electricity meters mass
memory, gross and net hourly generation data of the generation units referring to the previous month.
This data are compiled in a unique spreadsheet which is the HPP’s internal control generation
spreadsheet for the referred month under analysis.
Information of the internal control generation spreadsheet is checked with SCDE spreadsheets. However,
for emission reductions calculation, it was used the electricity generation data of CCEE General
Measurement Report, available in the CCEE website. This data consist of the net electricity generation
minus the losses of National Interconnected System (approximately 2.5%), setting, therefore, a more
conservative value.
Both generation information, internally generated and through CCEE website, are electronically stored by
the Operation and Maintenance Manager.
Periodically, the Information Technology Area makes a backup for all plant data through backup tapes.
All data collected as part of the monitoring was archived and will be kept for at least 2 years after the end
of the last crediting period.
All the information related to the total electricity produced by the project activity, including the electricity
supplied to the grid and the electricity supplied to internal loads, in year y (MWh), the net energy supplied
to the grid and the calculation of the emission factors were checked by the validation team, according to
the monitoring plan.
The verification team had access and verified the Calibration Certificates of all the energy measurement
equipments.
The emission reductions for the second monitoring period were calculated as follow:
EF = EF X W + EF X W (Equation 1)
grid,CM,y grid,OM,y OM grid,BM,y BM
Where:
EF = Build margin CO2 emission factor in year y (tCO2e/ MWh)
grid,BM,y
EF = Operating Margin CO2 emission in year y (tCO2e/ MWh)
grid,OM,y
W = Weighting of operating margin emissions factor (%) (0.5, as per tool for emission factor
OM
calculation)
W = Weighting of build margin emissions factor (%) (0.5, as per tool for emission factor calculation)
BM
PEy = EFRes * TEGy / 1000 (Equation 2)

Where:
PEy = Emission from reservoir expressed as tCO2e/year;
EFRes = is the default emission factor for emissions from reservoirs, and the default value as per EB23 is
90 Kg CO2e/MWh;
TEGy = Total electricity produced by the project activity, including the electricity supplied to the grid and
the electricity supplied to internal loads, in year y (MWh).
ERy = BEy – PEy (Equation 3)
Where:
ERy = Emission Reduction in year y (t CO2e/yr)
BEy = Baseline emissions in year y (t CO2e/yr)
PEy = Project emissions in year y (t CO2e/yr)
BEy = (EGy – EGbaseline) * EFgrid,CM,y (Equation 4)
Where:
BEy = Baseline Emission in year y (t CO2e/year)
EGy = Electricity supplied by the project activity to the grid (MWh)
EGbaseline = Baseline electricity supplied to the grid in case of modified or retrofit facilities (MWh). For
new power plants this value is taken as zero.
EFgrid,CM,y = Combined margin CO2 emission factor for grid connected power generation in year y
calculated using the latest version of the “Tool to calculate the emission factor for an electricity system”.
The two parameters (EGy - Electricity Supplied to the Grid – Net Electricity and EF - Combined

4.4 Management and Operational System
As presented in the Monitoring Report v04, section 3.3:
" HPP Barra Grande Operation and Maintenance Manager is responsible for the operation and
maintenance activities of the plant and for the activities of consolidation and analysis of gross and net
electricity. Measurements activities are carried out by the operators of the operation team".
Furthermore, the PP has explained that: "Project Participants performs cross-check of electricity
generation information and emission reductions calculations. BAESA has internal auditing process, once
the company is certified ISO 9001 and ISO 14001. However, the company did not establish a formal
procedure for emission reduction monitoring".
